title: Domain -> Caddy (VPS) -> Tailscale -> Home server (tsbridge) -> Jellyfin
author: wiseac
body:
Hello,
Im trying to get tsbridge to work with caddy to route public traffic through my vps into my home server. I have previously used just a host tailscale and then tagged certain ports for access (VPS (tailscale:8096) -> home server (tailscale:8096)). Ive wanted to get into sidecar method and saw tsbridge so i gave it a try. My current issue is that tsbridge creates the routes that and i can access from my tailnet but when it try and use caddy with the magicDNS name nothing gets through. I can ping the machine from the vps so i know its working. To my understanding it seems like some tls or cert failure. 

Best,

title: Possible to use tsbridge without putting all containers on the same network?
author: captain-reginald
body:
First of all, I want to say thank you for making this. I've loved another project called TSDproxy for a year now, but it seems to be semi-abandoned now and various features are no longer working. With Tailscale's recent price changes, my workflow now depends on OAuth which you support but seems to be broken in TSDproxy. This has saved me from having to spin up sidecars for each service.

Now to the question. The one thing that really bothers me about this setup is the fact that all containers must be on the same docker network to function with tsbridge. If any of them are public at all, this seems to come with security risks. I also noticed that because they're communicating internally, I need to point tsbridge at the internal ports of my service container, not the external ones that I would use if connecting directly through the host instead of the magicDNS name.

Is there some sort of limitation that doesn't allow simply having tsbridge listen on the public external ports instead and therefore remove the requirement for internal networking? I would assume that it's possible if the main container is already listening on /var/run/docker.sock.

If this isn't doable for technical reasons, I totally understand, just thought I'd ask what the thought is behind the way it's done.

title: Tsnet connection seems unstable
author: dcardin
body:
When tsbridge comes up, it tries to connect my services to my tailnet and I get errors on some of them :

> failed to start service myservice: resource error: creating listener: resource error: operation timeout: starting tsnet server for service \\\"myservice\\\" (check network connectivity to Tailscale) timed out after 30s",
>   "operation": "reload_add",

However, my tailnet is quite stable, no connection issues to tailscale at all. On the admin page, I see the container/service brought up, connected but the connection drops and then it will not come up again.

Any setting I can use to mitigate? is that an issue with tsbridge currently?

Thanks!

title: Can't get real client IP through Funnel
author: Ithvr
body:
I currently have a setup where I have tsbridge to make funnel connection to my internal reverse proxy.
For security reasons I have enabled x-forwarding these connections, but it seems to me that the client_ip is the ones  in the Tailscale network.

I may be misconfiguring something, but when Funnel traffic reaches my backend through tsbridge the  IP visible in X-Forwarded-For is a Tailscale range IP. I also know Funnel can do this since I did work in my former sidecar setup.

Here are some config examples:
```
[[services]]
name = "jellyfin"
backend_addr = "192.168.xxx.xxx:80"
funnel_enabled = true
trusted_proxies = ["100.64.0.0/10", "127.0.0.1", "fd7a:115c:a1e0::/48"]

[[services]]
name = "immich"
backend_addr = "192.168.xxx.xxx:2283"
funnel_enabled = true
trusted_proxies = ["100.64.0.0/10", "127.0.0.1", "fd7a:115c:a1e0::/48"]
```

Some of the logs from my caddy server (with my local IP and TS domain censored)

title: Stale State Folders Prevent Startup
author: Jcambass
body:
# Symptom 

tsbridge timeouts on starting `ts.Server` for the specific service.

# Reproduction

1. Configure a few services (machines) in the tsbridge config
1. Run tsbridge to ensure the `state` folders for each service have been created and the "service-machines" are connectd to tailscale
1. Shutdown tsbridge
1. Delete the "service-machines" from tailscale itself using the admin UI
1. Delete the services from the tsbridge config
1. Run tsbridge (probably not needed)
1. Restore the old config or at least re-add a few services that we just deleted
1. Try to run tsbridge again
1. Observe "service-machines" **not** being registered with tailscale and finding a log entry like

```
time=2026-02-12T21:42:19.422Z level=WARN msg="tsnet server start timed out" service=<your service> timeout=30s duration=30.001126769s
```

# Workaround

Manually delete the state folders for the affected services.

# Fix

I have to confess that I have not looked at the tsbridge code yet due to the lack of time. But based on my own experience with `tsnet`, it definitely possible to recover from the machine being deleted on the tailnet without fully re-creating the state folders.

title: tsbridge integration with multiple services with the same internal port possible?
author: ahanusch30
body:
I have a vm with some docker compose services deployed. Some Services use port 80 as internal port. In my case searXNG, seafile and filebrowser quantum.

As i understood in the label configuration of the different services i have to set the tsbridge.service.port, which is always the internal port of the docker image, not the port that is mapped to the host. I guess the port must be unique within my external network:
networks:
  tsbridge-network:
    external: true

My question now is if it is possible to deploy the three mentioned services within on VM, all with port 80 internal and different service.names?

If it is possible how would the configuration looks like. Your documentation says nothing to that setup.


Seafile:
seafile:
    image: ${SEAFILE_IMAGE:-seafileltd/seafile-mc:13.0-latest}
    container_name: seafile
    labels:
      #tsdproxy.enable: "true"
      #tsdproxy.name: files
      #tsdproxy.funnel: "true"
      tsbridge.enabled: "true"
      tsbridge.service.name: file
      tsbridge.service.port: 80
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - 80:80


Wordpress:
wordpress:
    image: wordpress:latest
    labels:
      tsbridge.enabled: "true"
      tsbridge.service.name: wordpress
      tsbridge.service.port: 80
      tsbridge.service.funnel_enabled: "true"
    volumes:
      - /opt/stacks/wordpress/wp_data:/var/www/html
    networks:
      - tsbridge-network
    ports:
      - 80:80

title: Not all services are being created
author: auspex
body:
I have, by `tsbridge`'s account, 15 tailscale services:
```
time=2026-01-18T04:10:20.162Z level=INFO msg="starting tsbridge" version=0.13.0 provider=docker
time=2026-01-18T04:10:20.163Z level=INFO msg="Docker provider initialized successfully" endpoint=unix:///var/run/docker.sock label_prefix=tsbridge poll_interval=1m0s

time=2026-01-18T04:10:20.178Z level=INFO msg="starting services"

time=2026-01-18T04:10:20.635Z level=INFO msg="started service" service=cvmc-books

time=2026-01-18T04:10:21.801Z level=INFO msg="started service" service=radarr
time=2026-01-18T04:10:21.968Z level=INFO msg="started service" service=prowlarr
time=2026-01-18T04:10:22.401Z level=INFO msg="started service" service=jellyfin
time=2026-01-18T04:11:20.196Z level=INFO msg="Docker configuration loaded successfully" services=15 label_prefix=tsbridge
```
but as you can see, only 9 of those were enumerated on startup. 4 more _have_ at some point worked—the "machines" show in the tailscale console, but as "offline"—but two more simply don't exist. 

If I restart one of the docker services that tsbridge did not proxy:
```
time=2026-01-18T04:21:04.181Z level=INFO msg="removed service associated with stopped container" container=calibre remaining_services=14

time=2026-01-18T04:21:04.269Z level=INFO msg="Docker configuration loaded successfully" services=14 label_prefix=tsbridge
```
Then slightly later:
```
time=2026-01-18T04:21:05.775Z level=INFO msg="Docker configuration loaded successfully" services=15 label_prefix=tsbridge
```
but it still never appears in tailscale (and there's none of the messages I'm used to that show the port it's proxying to).

How can I debug why the services don't start?

title: tsbridge flipping between two different ports
author: auspex
body:
While investigating another problem (mine, not tsbridge's), I found this in the `tsbridge` log:
```
time=2025-12-24T15:51:57.830Z level=INFO msg="updated service" service=jellyfin old_backend=jellyfin:8096 new_backend=jellyfin:8920
time=2025-12-24T15:51:57.830Z level=INFO msg="updated service during reload" service=jellyfin operation=reload_update backend=jellyfin:8920
time=2025-12-24T15:51:57.830Z level=INFO msg="configuration reload completed successfully" operations=2
time=2025-12-24T15:52:29.448Z level=INFO msg="Docker configuration loaded successfully" services=14 label_prefix=tsbridge
time=2025-12-24T15:52:29.449Z level=INFO msg="configuration changed, reloading services"
time=2025-12-24T15:52:29.450Z level=INFO msg="configuration changes detected" services_to_remove=0 services_to_add=0 services_to_update=1
time=2025-12-24T15:52:29.546Z level=INFO msg="updated service" service=jellyfin old_backend=jellyfin:8920 new_backend=jellyfin:8096
time=2025-12-24T15:52:29.546Z level=INFO msg="updated service during reload" service=jellyfin operation=reload_update backend=jellyfin:8096
time=2025-12-24T15:52:29.546Z level=INFO msg="configuration reload completed successfully" operations=1
time=2025-12-24T15:53:29.443Z level=INFO msg="Docker configuration loaded successfully" services=14 label_prefix=tsbridge
time=2025-12-24T15:53:29.444Z level=INFO msg="configuration changed, reloading services"
time=2025-12-24T15:53:29.445Z level=INFO msg="configuration changes detected" services_to_remove=0 services_to_add=0 services_to_update=1
time=2025-12-24T15:53:29.538Z level=INFO msg="updated service" service=jellyfin old_backend=jellyfin:8096 new_backend=jellyfin:8920
time=2025-12-24T15:53:29.538Z level=INFO msg="updated service during reload" service=jellyfin operation=reload_update backend=jellyfin:8920
```

I had failed to specify `tsbridge.service.port` for the container (I didn't even know it used port 8920!) and so it's flipping back and forth between two ports.

While the fix is obvious, this doesn't seem like desirable behavior. Shouldn't it either select one port and stick to it, or just complain that it _can't_ determine the port to use and force the user to specify it with `service.port`? [I think the latter is probably both simpler and more desirable]

title: Internal connection via domain name not possible
author: Tarow
body:
I am trying to run `tsbridge` using rootless Podman Quadlets.

All my applications are on the same Podman network. Now for OIDC to work, some apps will attempt to open a connection to my identity provider (Authelia) using the issuer URL. The request is routed through `tsbridge` but doesn't seem to be handled correctly. As a result the applications won't be able to start.

Example:
>  podman exec tsbridge wget -O- authelia.example.ts.net
> Connecting to authelia.taimen-manta.ts.net (10.81.0.24:80)
> wget: can't connect to remote host (10.81.0.24): Connection refused

Meanwhile using the container hostname, the connection will work:
> podman exec tsbridge wget -O- authelia:9091 > /dev/null
> Connecting to authelia:9091 (10.81.0.22:9091)
> writing to stdout
> \-                    100% |********************************|  1123  0:00:00 ETA
> written to stdout

Connections via the full domain `authelia.example.ts.net` work fine from external devices, e.g. my phone.
`tsbridge` seems to have a problem reverse proxying those requests when they come from the internal podman network.

---

I am running Podman 5.4.1 and tsbridge 0.13.0

title: Buffering - Stremio
author: keonramses
body:
Hi @jtdowney, I am having buffering issues when using AIOstream a Stremio addon. The vm has both IPV4 & IPV6 addresses and when I view the connection in tailscale, the connection shows as direct but I have constant buffering. Thank you.

```yml

tsbridge:
    image: ghcr.io/jtdowney/tsbridge:v0.12.3
    container_name: tsbridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    command:
      - --provider
      - docker
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock
      - ${DOCKER_DATA_DIR}/tsbridge:/var/lib/tsbridge
    environment:
      - TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=${TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}
      - TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=${TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET}
    labels:
      - tsbridge.tailscale.oauth_client_id_env=TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID
      - tsbridge.tailscale.oauth_client_secret_env=TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET
      - tsbridge.tailscale.state_dir=/var/lib/tsbridge
      - tsbridge.tailscale.default_tags=tag:rockydocker # Must match or be owned by your OAuth client's tag
    networks:
      - backend

aiostreams-ts:
    image: ghcr.io/viren070/aiostreams:${AIOSTREAMS_TAG:-latest}
    container_name: aiostreams-ts
    restart: unless-stopped
    expose:
      - 3000
    env_file:
      - app_env/aiostreams_ts.env
    volumes:
      - ${DOCKER_DATA_DIR}/aiostreams:/app/data
    networks:
      - frontend
      - backend
    labels:
      - tsbridge.enabled=true
      - tsbridge.service.name=aio
      - tsbridge.service.port=3000
      # Streaming Optimizations
      - tsbridge.service.write_timeout=0s
      - tsbridge.service.flush_interval=-1ms
      # Funnel
      - tsbridge.service.funnel_enabled=true

```

title: Support for Docker Socket Proxy via DOCKER_HOST environment variable
author: MichielMak
body:
**Description:**

### Problem
Currently, tsbridge requires a direct mount of `/var/run/docker.sock` to discover Docker containers via labels. This creates security concerns, as it grants the container full access to the Docker daemon.

Many users run a Docker socket proxy (like [linuxserver/socket-proxy](https://github.com/linuxserver/docker-socket-proxy) or Tecnativa's proxy) to provide limited, read-only access to specific Docker API endpoints. This is considered a security best practice for label-based discovery tools.

### Proposed Solution
Add support for the `DOCKER_HOST` environment variable to allow tsbridge to connect to a Docker socket proxy over TCP instead of requiring a direct socket mount.

**Example usage:**
```yaml
services:
  tsbridge:
    image: ghcr.io/jtdowney/tsbridge:latest
    command: ["--provider", "docker"]
    environment:
      - DOCKER_HOST=tcp://dockerproxy:2375
      - TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID=${TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}
      - TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET=${TS_OAUTH_CLIENT_SECRET}
```
### Benefits
- **Security**: Limits Docker API access to only what's needed (containers, services, tasks)
- **Best practices**: Aligns with security recommendations for production deployments
- **Compatibility**: Works with existing socket proxy setups used by other monitoring/discovery tools

### Reference Implementations
Other tools that support this pattern:
- Diun (via `DIUN_PROVIDERS_DOCKER_ENDPOINT`)
- Traefik (via `DOCKER_HOST`)
- Watchtower (via `DOCKER_HOST`)

title: Adding a dashboard to display all the services created by tsbridge
author: GustavoCaso
body:
I find very useful to have a simple dashboard for accessing to all my containers on my tailnet. I was wondering @jtdowney if you would be open to the idea of that for tsbridge. 

I would be more than happy to work on adding such feature to tsbridge, still I wanted to ask first to ensure you are open to the idea 😄
